steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content:
A linear equation has no solution when the variable terms are the same on both sides but the constants are different. First expand the left side:
$$4(kx+11)=4kx+44$$

steps.title: Match the variable terms
steps.content:
The equation becomes
$$4kx+44=\frac{60}{19}x+40$$
For the variable terms to match, set the coefficients equal:
$$4k=\frac{60}{19}$$

steps.title: Solve for the constant
steps.content:
Divide both sides by $4$:
$$k=\frac{60}{19}\div4=\frac{60}{76}=\frac{15}{19}$$
Then the constants $44$ and $40$ are unequal, so the equation has no solution. The answer is 15/19.
